entry.title: Queries on SAP LLM and Joule?
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We are looking to enable Joule. However before doing so, we want to understand more about following question. Please share your thoughts on these questions.

1. Which LLM model is SAP using? Is it homegrown or Other models like GPT, BERT or LLAMA?

2. How is SAP addressing the hallucination issues?

3. Any other terms and condition that SAP want Client to accept in order to use Gen AI/Joule?
4. Is same LLM used in various modules or different LLM used ? For example for Job description one LLM, For SF chatboat another LLM model?
5. What are the update cadence for the SAP LLM Product like schedule release or Adhoc release?

6 .Where does this LLM resides and size of custom SAP LLM?

7. Once Joule is enabled, can we turn On/Off depending on modules?

entry.title: What is up with Joule?
entry.content:
Anyone else find it frustrating how much sales material is out there on some of these new features but all real information and documentation is missing or deficient? The release notes on Joule do not at all make it clear when it will be available or what steps you will need to take to implement it

The whats new viewer directs you to a KBA and the KBA has two dead links to some page on SAP help. I would have expected a feature which was such a focus of SuccessConnect to have a better rollout at least on the information side. The MS teams integration documenation and information is also extremely deficient

This community change is also very frustrating. I don't a high expectation that anyone will even see this post. The engagement of the community since the change has been so much lower. Is anyone else frustrated with SAP's communication and engagement related to product features which are conversely so meticiously sold to new customers? We all get executives seeing these new product features and demanding that they are implemented, and it makes us look foolish when we have no idea how to actually do that or provide realistic expectations

entry.title: How to Enable Joule in Preview
entry.content:
Has anyone been successful in enable joule via customer config? I've read the KBA and the instructions say "Enable Joule". I've not been successful in locating additional detailed instructions outlining how to complete the customer config.

Is Joule required to be turned on somehow in IAS before setting permissions in preview?

entry.title: Hello,Do we need to purchase or have a license to enable the AI Joule bot?
entry.content:
We are interested in enabling the Joule bot in our preview instance, but unsure if we need to purchase that functionality or purchase a license to be able to use it, or if it comes with something we have already purchase (i.e Employee Central).

If anyone knows, or can provide any documentation on how to enable, if you have done so, I would appreciate the help.   The documentation I found says it should show in 'general user permissions' in RBP, but I don't see it there, so maybe we do need to purchase something?

Thank you in advance,

Madison Ruff

entry.title: Training the SAP AI Model with SAP Help Documents
entry.content:
Hi All,

I have a requirement for building a chatbot that is trained with SAP Internal Help document.

I have a parent link and in turn the parent link navigates to various sub links.

We want our AI model to be trained with all the data available from the parent link and the sub links.

How can we achieve this ? is there an out of the box integration techniques available for this purpose ?

Thanks,

Santhosini

entry.title: Testing Joule in BTP Trial
entry.content:
Hi,
Does SAP Joule will be available to test on BTP Trial please?

Thanks,
Mauricio Predolim

entry.title: SAP Development context : Github Copilot Vs Joule
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

With the introduction of Joule in the SAP development context, is there a significance/impact with Github Copilot?

I believe Github Copilot is general, whereas Joule is extensively trained with the latest SAP innovations.

What additional benefits Github-Colipot can bring in the SAP S/4 HANA development context?

Best wishes,

Ramjee Korada
